title: Tetracera aspera

This plant is native to tropical Asia and is widely distributed across various parts of the continent. The classification of Tetracera aspera is further broken down into the kingdom Plantae and the order Dilleniales. intro: Tetracera aspera, also known as the rough-stemmed Tetracera, is a plant species in the family Dilleniaceae, native to Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which provide potential health benefits such as wound healing and immune system support. Bioactive constituents of Tetracera aspera include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Tetracera aspera is often used in traditional herbal preparations, including decoctions, infusions, and ointments, to harness its medicinal benefits and treat various health issues.
